blockingIssues.description: `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` still reaps stale lease runs by joining expired leases to runs on `runId` alone, so a malformed foreign-company lease row can still cancel another company's run.

`ui/src/components/IssuesList.tsx` still filters, sorts, and groups by raw `issue.status`, so recovered stale work can remain visible under active issue buckets even when row rendering shows recovered/todo state.

The recorded regression loop used curl for approval and heartbeat mutations, so it did not prove a real TUI management action against the demo work and does not satisfy the intended VAL-CROSS-002 coverage.
New sidebar management shortcuts remain live behind help/settings overlays, allowing hidden mutating actions while an overlay covers the main panel.
Approval polling failures are rendered as `No pending approvals` instead of an error, making the shortcut-based approval flow unreliable and not operator-visible when its API call fails.
`server/src/services/work-products.ts` dedupes artifacts using `createdByRunId`, so persisted artifact rows with a null run id can still duplicate a derived fallback for the same concrete artifact.
`server/src/__tests__/issue-comment-reopen-routes.test.ts` now invokes only the final route handler, bypassing validation middleware and weakening the real HTTP route-contract coverage.
`server/src/__tests__/routines-routes.test.ts` likewise bypasses the real middleware stack, so the suite can miss validator and route-wiring regressions while staying deterministic.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax the mandatory tests-first/red sequence for partial hardening and flake-stabilization follow-ups, while still requiring targeted regression coverage and full validators before handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `stale-recovery-backend-hardening` and `stabilize-remaining-full-suite-flakes` recorded justified deviations from the current tests-first step because the work required reproduction/gap analysis against already-landed code before tightening the fix.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when TUI validation is mandatory and document an acceptable fallback for mutation-oriented TUI assertions when `tuistory` text entry is flaky or when a feature changes no TUI behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `full-demo-repo-regression-loop` fell back to curl/browser mutations and missed the intended TUI-management assertion, while `harden-onboarding-workspace-seed-files` was a non-TUI fix that still had to record a skill deviation because the procedure unconditionally required `tuistory` checks.
